# Step 6 — Artifact Suppression

> **Milestone**: M1.4 | **Epic**: E1.4.4 | **Feature**: F1.4.4.1
> **Depends on**: nothing (parallel with Step 5 — zero shared files)
> **Skills to load**: `flutter-handling-concurrency`, `flutter-testing-apps`

Load shared context: [M1.4 reading polish context](../instructions/m1.4-reading-polish-context.instructions.md)

---

## Task

Create a pure-Dart artifact classifier that detects tables, code blocks, and figure references in extracted document text. These artifacts make no sense in word-at-a-time RSVP — they need to be flagged so they can be displayed differently or skipped. Also create a suppression overlay for users to mark regions during paused reading.

## Files to Create

### 1. `lib/features/reading/pacing/artifact_classifier.dart`

This is **pure Dart** — no Flutter imports. It must be safe to run in `Isolate.run()`.

```dart
/// Classifies regions of extracted text that are likely non-prose artifacts
/// (tables, code blocks, figure captions, page numbers) which degrade the
/// RSVP reading experience.
///
/// Designed to run in an isolate — no Flutter or FFI dependencies.
library;

/// A detected artifact region within a word list.
class ArtifactRegion {
  /// Creates an artifact region spanning [startIndex] to [endIndex] inclusive.
  const ArtifactRegion({
    required this.startIndex,
    required this.endIndex,
    required this.type,
    required this.confidence,
  });

  /// Index of first word in the artifact (inclusive).
  final int startIndex;

  /// Index of last word in the artifact (inclusive).
  final int endIndex;

  /// The type of artifact detected.
  final ArtifactType type;

  /// Detection confidence from 0.0 (guess) to 1.0 (certain).
  final double confidence;

  /// Number of words in this region.
  int get length => endIndex - startIndex + 1;
}

/// Types of artifacts that can be detected.
enum ArtifactType {
  /// Tabular data — columns, delimiters, repeated numeric patterns.
  table,

  /// Code blocks — braces, semicolons, indentation patterns.
  codeBlock,

  /// Figure/table captions — "Figure 1:", "Table 2:", etc.
  caption,

  /// Page numbers and headers/footers.
  pageMarker,

  /// Bibliographic references — "[1]", "(Author, 2023)", etc.
  reference,
}
```

**Detection heuristics:**

```dart
/// Scan a word list and return detected artifact regions.
///
/// This function is isolate-safe — call via `Isolate.run(() => classifyArtifacts(words))`.
List<ArtifactRegion> classifyArtifacts(List<String> words) {
  final regions = <ArtifactRegion>[];

  // 1. Table detection: consecutive words that are numbers, pipes, dashes
  //    Look for windows where >60% of words match [0-9.|_-]+
  regions.addAll(_detectTables(words));

  // 2. Code block detection: windows with high density of {, }, ;, =, (), //
  regions.addAll(_detectCodeBlocks(words));

  // 3. Caption detection: "Figure N:", "Table N:", "Fig. N"
  regions.addAll(_detectCaptions(words));

  // 4. Page marker detection: isolated numbers (likely page numbers)
  regions.addAll(_detectPageMarkers(words));

  // 5. Reference detection: "[N]" patterns, "(Author, YYYY)"
  regions.addAll(_detectReferences(words));

  // Merge overlapping regions
  return _mergeOverlapping(regions);
}
```

**Individual detectors — signatures:**

```dart
List<ArtifactRegion> _detectTables(List<String> words) {
  // Sliding window of 10 words
  // If ≥6/10 match pattern: all digits, pipe-delimited, dash-only, or single chars
  // Extend window while pattern holds
  // Confidence: (matching count / window size)
}

List<ArtifactRegion> _detectCodeBlocks(List<String> words) {
  // Sliding window of 15 words
  // Count code signals: contains {, }, ;, //, /*, ->, =>, ==, !=, >=, <=
  // If ≥6/15 are code signals → code block
  // Extend while pattern holds
  // Confidence: 0.7 for heuristic, 0.9 if ``` markers found
}

List<ArtifactRegion> _detectCaptions(List<String> words) {
  // Match: word[i] case-insensitively matches "figure" | "fig." | "table" | "chart"
  // AND word[i+1] matches a number pattern
  // Region: i to min(i + 15, words.length - 1) — captions are short
  // Confidence: 0.85
}

List<ArtifactRegion> _detectPageMarkers(List<String> words) {
  // Isolated single number (1–4 digits) surrounded by non-numeric words
  // AND not part of a sentence (no lowercase words adjacent)
  // Region: just the one word
  // Confidence: 0.5 (low — may be a real number in text)
}

List<ArtifactRegion> _detectReferences(List<String> words) {
  // Match: "[N]" where N is 1-3 digits
  // Or: "(Author, YYYY)" pattern — word starts with "(", contains comma, ends with ")"
  // Region: the matched words
  // Confidence: 0.75
}

List<ArtifactRegion> _mergeOverlapping(List<ArtifactRegion> regions) {
  // Sort by startIndex, merge any regions that overlap or are adjacent
  // When merging, keep the higher-confidence type
}
```

### 2. `lib/features/reading/widgets/suppression_overlay.dart`

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
import 'package:runthru/design/design.dart';
import 'package:runthru/features/reading/pacing/artifact_classifier.dart';

/// Overlay that highlights detected artifact regions during paused reading.
///
/// Shows detected artifact regions with type badges. Users can long-press
/// a region to toggle suppression (skip during reading). Only visible
/// when reading is paused — never during active RSVP.
class SuppressionOverlay extends ConsumerWidget {
  const SuppressionOverlay({
    super.key,
    required this.isPaused,
    required this.words,
    required this.regions,
    required this.suppressedIndices,
    required this.onToggleSuppression,
  });

  /// Whether reading is currently paused.
  final bool isPaused;

  /// The word list for display context.
  final List<String> words;

  /// Detected artifact regions.
  final List<ArtifactRegion> regions;

  /// Set of word indices currently marked for suppression.
  final Set<int> suppressedIndices;

  /// Callback when user toggles suppression on a region.
  final void Function(ArtifactRegion region) onToggleSuppression;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context, WidgetRef ref) { ... }
}
```

**Design:**
- Only visible when `isPaused` is true
- List of detected regions with:
  - Type badge (icon + label): `Icons.table_chart` (table), `Icons.code` (code), `Icons.image` (caption), `Icons.bookmark` (page marker), `Icons.format_quote` (reference)
  - Short preview of the region text (first ~5 words + "…")
  - Toggle button: "Skip" / "Include" (never color-only — use icon + label)
  - `Semantics` label: "Detected {type}: {preview}. Tap to {skip|include}."
- Positioned as a scrollable panel at the bottom of the reading screen
- Background: `RunThruTokens.stageBase` with 95% opacity
- Text: `RunThruTypography.caption`
- Touch targets: ≥ 48dp on all interactive elements
- If no regions detected, show nothing (not an empty state)
- All badge icons paired with text labels (Rule 11: no color-only signals)

### 3. `test/features/reading/pacing/artifact_classifier_test.dart`

```dart
import 'package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart';
import 'package:runthru/features/reading/pacing/artifact_classifier.dart';
```

**Test groups:**

```dart
group('classifyArtifacts', () {
  test('returns empty list for plain prose', () {
    final words = 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog and runs away'.split(' ');
    final regions = classifyArtifacts(words);
    expect(regions, isEmpty);
  });

  test('detects table-like content', () {
    // Simulate extracted table: "Name | Age | City John | 30 | NYC"
    final words = ['Name', '|', 'Age', '|', 'City', 'John', '|', '30', '|', 'NYC'];
    final regions = classifyArtifacts(words);
    expect(regions, isNotEmpty);
    expect(regions.first.type, ArtifactType.table);
  });

  test('detects code blocks', () {
    final words = ['void', 'main()', '{', 'print(', '"hello"', ');', '}'];
    final regions = classifyArtifacts(words);
    expect(regions, isNotEmpty);
    expect(regions.first.type, ArtifactType.codeBlock);
  });

  test('detects figure captions', () {
    final words = ['Figure', '1:', 'The', 'relationship', 'between', 'variables'];
    final regions = classifyArtifacts(words);
    expect(regions, isNotEmpty);
    expect(regions.first.type, ArtifactType.caption);
  });

  test('detects page numbers', () {
    final words = ['end', 'of', 'chapter.', '42', 'The', 'next', 'chapter'];
    final regions = classifyArtifacts(words);
    final pageMarkers = regions.where((r) => r.type == ArtifactType.pageMarker);
    expect(pageMarkers, isNotEmpty);
  });

  test('detects bracketed references', () {
    final words = ['as', 'shown', 'by', '[1]', 'and', '[2]', 'the'];
    final regions = classifyArtifacts(words);
    final refs = regions.where((r) => r.type == ArtifactType.reference);
    expect(refs, isNotEmpty);
  });

  test('merges adjacent regions', () {
    // Two adjacent table-like sections should merge into one
    final words = List.generate(20, (i) => i.isEven ? '|' : '$i');
    final regions = classifyArtifacts(words);
    // Should be merged into a single region, not multiple
    expect(regions.length, lessThanOrEqualTo(2));
  });

  test('returns correct indices', () {
    final words = ['Hello', 'world.', 'Figure', '1:', 'Caption', 'text.', 'More', 'prose.'];
    final regions = classifyArtifacts(words);
    final caption = regions.firstWhere((r) => r.type == ArtifactType.caption);
    expect(caption.startIndex, 2); // "Figure"
    expect(caption.endIndex, greaterThanOrEqualTo(3)); // at least "1:"
  });
});

group('ArtifactRegion', () {
  test('length is calculated correctly', () {
    const region = ArtifactRegion(
      startIndex: 5,
      endIndex: 10,
      type: ArtifactType.table,
      confidence: 0.8,
    );
    expect(region.length, 6);
  });
});
```

---

## What NOT to Do

- Do NOT add Flutter imports to `artifact_classifier.dart` — it must be isolate-safe
- Do NOT run classification on the main thread — the caller (reading screen) should use `Isolate.run()`
- Do NOT modify `WordTimerNotifier` — suppression affects what words are *displayed*, not timer logic
- Do NOT modify `lib/core/orp.dart`, `lib/three_d/`, or gesture code
- Do NOT over-engineer detection — these are heuristics, not ML. False negatives are acceptable; false positives on real prose are not.
- Do NOT create a Riverpod provider for suppression state yet — pass it as widget state (the reading screen integration comes when Step 5 is wired up)
- Do NOT use raw `Color(0xFF...)` or raw `TextStyle` in the overlay widget

---

## Verification

```powershell
dart analyze lib/features/reading/pacing/artifact_classifier.dart lib/features/reading/widgets/suppression_overlay.dart
flutter test test/features/reading/pacing/artifact_classifier_test.dart
```

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ArtifactRegion` class exists with `startIndex`, `endIndex`, `type`, `confidence`, `length`
- [ ] `ArtifactType` enum: `table`, `codeBlock`, `caption`, `pageMarker`, `reference`
- [ ] `classifyArtifacts(List<String>)` returns `List<ArtifactRegion>`
- [ ] Function is pure Dart — no Flutter imports, isolate-safe
- [ ] Table detection works for pipe-delimited and numeric-heavy content
- [ ] Code block detection works for brace/semicolon patterns
- [ ] Caption detection catches "Figure N:", "Table N:", "Fig. N"
- [ ] Page marker detection catches isolated numbers with low confidence
- [ ] Reference detection catches "[N]" patterns
- [ ] Overlapping/adjacent regions are merged
- [ ] Plain prose returns empty list (no false positives)
- [ ] `SuppressionOverlay` widget exists with type badges (icon + label, not color-only)
- [ ] Overlay only visible when paused
- [ ] All touch targets ≥ 48dp
- [ ] All `Semantics` labels present on interactive elements
- [ ] All colors from `RunThruTokens`, all text from `RunThruTypography`
- [ ] Test file covers: empty input, prose, tables, code, captions, page markers, references, merging, index correctness
- [ ] All tests pass: `flutter test test/features/reading/pacing/artifact_classifier_test.dart`
- [ ] `dart analyze` — zero issues on all created files
- [ ] All public symbols have dartdoc comments
